skill.name: tdd-workflows-tdd-cycle
skill.description: Comprehensive Test-Driven Development workflow with strict red-green-refactor discipline
skill.summary: Execute TDD workflows using agent orchestration with configurable coverage thresholds and continuous validation
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 15 static analysis findings are false positives. The external_commands pattern matched documentation references (opening markdown files, flag examples) not actual shell execution. Cryptographic and reconnaissance patterns matched unrelated TDD workflow text. This skill safely orchestrates Claude Task agents for test-driven development workflows.

low_findings.title: External Command Pattern - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer detected Ruby/shell backtick execution pattern. Line 23 references opening a markdown documentation file. Lines 134 and 144 show command-line flag examples (--incremental, --suite). No actual shell execution occurs.

content.value_statement: Developers struggle to maintain TDD discipline while managing complex projects. This skill orchestrates specialized agents through strict red-green-refactor cycles with automated validation and coverage tracking.
actual_capabilities: Orchestrates multi-agent TDD workflow through six structured phases
Enforces strict red-green-refactor discipline with gate validations
Configures coverage thresholds (80% line, 75% branch, 100% critical path)
Supports both incremental test-by-test and comprehensive test suite modes
Tracks TDD metrics including phase timing and defect escape rates
Provides failure recovery with rollback to valid states
limitations: Requires access to Claude Task tool with subagent capabilities
Depends on external test framework integration for execution
Not suitable for projects without automated testing infrastructure
Coverage thresholds may need adjustment for legacy codebases

use_cases.title: Greenfield Feature Development
use_cases.description: Develop new features test-first with strict TDD discipline, ensuring 100% coverage from the start.
use_cases.target_user: Software engineers building new modules or features

use_cases.title: Legacy Code Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Add comprehensive test coverage to existing code using the test suite mode with batch development.

use_cases.title: Code Quality Improvement
use_cases.description: Refactor existing code while maintaining test coverage, using automated validation at each step.

prompt_templates.title: Basic TDD Cycle
prompt_templates.prompt: Start a TDD workflow for implementing a user authentication module. Begin with Phase 1 requirements analysis and proceed through the red-green-refactor cycle.

prompt_templates.title: Incremental Test Development
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute TDD workflow for a REST API endpoint with --incremental flag. Write one failing test at a time, make it pass, then refactor before proceeding.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Test Suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute TDD workflow for a payment processing module with --suite flag. Write all failing tests first, then implement to pass all tests, then refactor the entire module.

prompt_templates.title: Coverage Improvement
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze current test coverage for the user service module and execute Phase 11 to add performance tests and edge case coverage. Target 90% branch coverage.

output_examples.input: TDD workflow for user registration API
output_examples.output: Phase 1 Complete: Test specification generated with 12 test scenarios covering happy path, validation errors, duplicate users, and edge cases. Phase 2 Complete: 12 failing unit tests written. Phase 3 Complete: Minimal implementation passes all tests. Phase 4 Complete: Refactored to reduce cyclomatic complexity from 12 to 6.

output_examples.input: TDD workflow with coverage analysis for data validation module
output_examples.output: Coverage Report: Line coverage 87%, Branch coverage 79%, Function coverage 92%. All tests passing. Refactoring recommendations: Extract validation logic into separate strategies, reduce method length in validateUserInput from 28 to 15 lines.

best_practices: Always verify tests fail for the right reasons before implementing code
Implement only the minimum code required to pass tests - no extra features
Run tests after every refactoring change to ensure tests remain green
Stop immediately if TDD discipline is broken and rollback to valid state
Keep test execution under 5 seconds for unit tests to maintain feedback speed
anti_patterns: Writing implementation code before writing failing tests
Writing tests that already pass instead of driving new functionality
Skipping the refactor phase and accumulating technical debt
Modifying tests to make them pass rather than fixing implementation
Ignoring failing tests and proceeding to next feature

faq.question: What is the red-green-refactor cycle?
faq.answer: RED: Write a failing test. GREEN: Implement minimum code to make it pass. REFACTOR: Improve code quality while keeping tests green. This cycle ensures test-first development with continuous quality improvement.

faq.question: How do I choose between incremental and suite mode?
faq.answer: Use incremental mode for complex logic requiring careful step-by-step development. Use suite mode for straightforward features where you can design all tests upfront and implement in batch.

faq.question: What happens if my tests fail during refactoring?
faq.answer: Stop immediately and rollback to the last valid state. Refactoring must not change behavior. If tests fail, you changed functionality rather than just improving structure.

faq.question: Can I adjust the coverage thresholds?
faq.answer: Yes. Default thresholds are 80% line coverage, 75% branch coverage, and 100% critical path coverage. Adjust based on project requirements and legacy code constraints.

faq.question: What tools does this skill require?
faq.answer: This skill requires the Claude Task tool with access to subagents for testing, code review, and backend development. It orchestrates specialized agents for each TDD phase.

faq.question: How does failure recovery work?
faq.answer: If TDD discipline is broken, stop immediately, identify the violated phase, rollback to the last valid state using version control, resume from the correct phase, and document the lesson learned.
